title: INP reflects main-thread responsiveness
description: Interaction to Next Paint is driven by long tasks blocking the main thread between an input and its visual response.
body: Use when an interaction feels sluggish despite a fast initial load. INP measures the latency from a user interaction to the next paint; high INP usually means long tasks (heavy JS, large synchronous renders, expensive event handlers) monopolize the main thread so the browser cannot paint. Do not assume a good LCP implies good interactivity. Actions: break up long tasks, yield with scheduling, and defer non-urgent work off the interaction path. Validation signals include a performance trace showing shorter tasks and a lower measured INP. Risk boundary: a single >50ms task on a hot interaction can dominate INP; profile the real handler rather than guessing.
